primary_factors: Temperatures well above freezing through the entire overnight + 6–8 AM bus window (low ~40°F; wind chills in the 30s°F) → no refreeze/ice mechanism
No snow/ice precipitation expected (0" snowfall / 0 precip signal) and good visibility forecast; no winter alerts
Road/infrastructure posture: roads reported clear with no plow activity needed; Michigan winter operations threshold not approached
News intelligence: Rockford Public Schools site shows no closure/delay banner; nearby districts (Sparta, Cedar Springs) appear normal with scheduled events; no community buzz
Power grid: Kent County outage footprint is essentially negligible (reported ~2 customers out); no outage-driven closure risk
decision_rationale:
All specialist inputs converge on a non-winter school day for Rockford, MI on Mon 2026-03-09: above-freezing temperatures all night and during bus hours, no accumulating snow/ice forecast, and no winter hazards (no blowing snow, no severe cold). The only ‘flag’ mentioned in earlier news intel was an NWS Dense Fog Advisory snapshot, but follow-up confirms that advisory was for Fri Mar 6 and is not evidence of a Mon Mar 9 commute hazard. Safety/infrastructure assessments align: roads should be bare/wet with only a routine watch for isolated damp/shaded spots; plow timing is irrelevant because no snow is expected. Power-grid follow-up indicates Kent County outages are minimal and decreasing, so there’s no plausible outage-driven closure.

Given Michigan’s high closure threshold and the absence of any closure triggers (ice, heavy snow during commute, blizzard conditions, extreme wind chills, or widespread outages), the rational operational call is OPEN. I keep a small 1% residual for rare non-weather contingencies or a highly localized surprise issue (e.g., a very isolated facility/outage problem), not for a true “snow day.”

Recommendation: OPEN with routine monitoring (no delay needed).

collaboration.summary: Agents reached consensus after 1 round(s) with 0.5% spread. Average probability: 1%.
